observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books to ensure accurate balances; and
b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of these accounts so that any errors and omissions could be resolved or adjusted in a timely manner.

a. Require the Collecting Officers to remit intact their full collections to the Treasurer at the close of each business day to lessen the risk of misuse and loss of government funds; Issue official receipts in strict numerical sequence, and strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 29 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs Vol. I and Section 7 of COA Circular 92-382 dated July 3, 1992;

b. Direct Municipal Treasurer to monitor the issuance of Accountable Forms and to refrain further issuance of additional Official Receipts to collectors unless remittances of all collections are made and previous accountable forms are clearly accounted in the RAAF.

a. Direct the GSO to initiate and undertake the necessary process of disposal of the duly accounted unserviceable properties following the revised manual on disposal prescribed in COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024; and

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary entries for the dropping of these assets from the books of accounts, once the disposal process is complete.

a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify tax collection strategies and to institute administrative and judicial remedies against delinquent taxpayers, if necessary; and

b. Direct the Local Finance Committee to set an attainable income estimate and ensure its collection, in order to meet the expenditure requirements of the LGU in a given year.
observations.reason: Records of the Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the OIC - Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to examine and reconcile their respective assessment data to generate a reliable and accurate assessment record/report; and

b. After reconciliation, direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card showing the name of tax payers and the amount due.

observations.reason: Still lack of manpower. Still in the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a. The OIC-Municipal General Services Officer to:
i. Maintain stock cards for each type of Inventory which shall be
reconciled with the SLC;
ii. Prepare the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS as
provided in Section 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 to
properly monitor the utilization of supplies and materials by requiring end-user
departments to submit monthly inventory reports showing receipts, issuances,
and ending balances; and
iii. Conduct the semestral physical count of inventory items by type for all
supplies and materials, prepare the RPCI, and submit to the Auditor concerned
not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively.
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Maintain the necessary perpetual inventory records including the SLC for
each commodity/stock in accordance with Section 114 of the Manual on the
NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1;
ii. Record purchases of supplies and materials for stock to their appropriate
inventory accounts, except those purchased through the petty cash fund, and
recognize as expense only when stocks are issued upon receipt of the SSMI
together with the original copy of the RIS as provided in Section 121 of the
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and
iii. Reconcile the RPCI after the conduct of periodic physical inventory of
supplies or properties to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of Inventory
account balances.

2.13 We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Recognize the corresponding municipal share for advance payments
under the Discount on Advance Payments account and credit the account when
the discount is applied;
b. Prepare the following adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of
the RPT and SET receivable and Due to LGU accounts; and
Debit Credit
Other Deferred Credits 683,110.89
Deferred Real Property Tax (40%) 151,714.64
Deferred Special Education Fund Tax 151,912.15
(50%)
Due to LGUs - Province 258,250.66
Due to LGUs - Barangays 75,956.08
Discount on Advance Payments 45,277.37
c. Ensure proper segregation and classification of all components of real
property tax collections, including current dues, advance payments, discounts,
and penalties at the point of recording.

3.9 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The Sangguniang Bayan enact the necessary resolutions formally
authorizing the operation of each LEE to establish a clear legal foundation and
align with the requirements of R.A. No. 7160 and the LEE Manual;
b. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant ensure that
separate Annual Operating Budgets (AOBs) are prepared and maintained for
each LEE, consistent with Item 5.1(f) of the LEE Manual, and to properly
integrate into the approved appropriation ordinance; and
c. The Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office (MEEDO), in
coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator
(MPDC), prepare a comprehensive 5-Year Business Plan for each existing
LEE, covering management, operations, staffing, risk, marketing, and
financial projections, to promote operational sustainability and compliance
with Item 5.3 of the LEE Manual.

4.9 We recommended and the Management agreed that:
a. The Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO), in
coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and approving
authorities, ensure that all Job Order contracts are prepared, reviewed, and
approved prior to the actual engagement of personnel, with complete
signatures and dates to establish its enforceability and validity;
b. The HRMO ensure that all Job Order contracts clearly define the duties,
responsibilities, and expected deliverables of hired personnel to provide a clear
basis for monitoring and evaluating actual services rendered;
c. The HRMO, in coordination with the heads of offices where Job Order
personnel are assigned, require that duly signed individual accomplishment
reports be submitted by Job Order personnel and their immediate supervisors
to support payroll processing and validate that services rendered are consistent
with the agreed terms of engagement;
d. The Municipal Accountant verify that all disbursement vouchers for Job
Order personnel are supported with complete documentation, including duly
approved contracts and accomplishment reports, prior to processing and
recording of payments; and
e. Furthermore, Management should strengthen internal controls and establish
clear guidelines on the hiring, documentation, and monitoring of Job Order
personnel to safeguard accountability, uphold management authority, and
ensure the proper and efficient use of government resources.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the write-off of the outstanding dormant account amounting to ₱95,571.32 pursuant to the specific guidelines and procedures as provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

a. Direct the GSO to initiate and undertake the necessary process of disposal of the duly accounted unserviceable properties following the procedures prescribed in COA Circular 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and 
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary entries for the dropping of these assets from the books of accounts, once the disposal process is complete.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
 a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a reconciliation report of the
 reciprocal accounts and take up necessary adjustments in the books to ensure
 accurate balances; and
 b. Conduct periodic reconciliation of these accounts so that any errors and
 omissions could be resolved or adjusted in a timely manner.

2.8 Hence, we recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to initiate the write-off of the long outstanding dormant account
amounting to ₱95,571.32 pursuant to the specific guidelines and procedures
as provided in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.
Strictly adhere to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG- CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated especially on the allowable expenditures, programs, projects, and activities to be charged in the LDRRMF.

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and other concerned implementing office to undertake identified PPAs within the planned time-frame and subsequently, closely monitor, evaluate the progress, and supervise the implementation of both infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs, funded under the 20 percent Development Fund to avoid delays in the completion of targeted projects; and
b. Establish a system of monitoring, gathering, and consolidating all data/information on the status of implementation of all PPAs undertaken by the Municipal Government and designate a focal person/office/department to aid the Local Chief Executive in exercising its mandate on general supervision and control overall programs, projects, services, and activities of the LGU.
b. Direct the GSO to initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties as listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant, after receiving necessary documents, to record the dropping off of the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts;
d. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to book up the donated properties at fair value;

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Establish an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members
to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the Accounting
and Property Divisions/Units of the agency;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee to come up with a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP)
with timeframe for its one-time cleansing of Property, Plant and Equipment
(PPE) accounts. The Inventory Committee shall be guided by the procedures
provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006;
c. Direct the Inventory Committee to reconcile the discrepancy between the
balances in the books and per physical count of the PPE and effect appropriate
adjustments if necessary;
d. Direct the GSO to initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable
properties as listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable
Property (IIRUP);
e. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the dropping off of the unserviceable
properties from the books of accounts after receiving required documents;
f. Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to regularly reconcile their
respective property and ledger cards;
g. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct regularly a year-end physical
inventory on Property, Plant and Equipment and submit it to the Accounting
Unit for reconciliation of balances to ensure correctness and reliability of the
PPE account balances; and
h. Provide additional manpower to assist the GSO in performing the rigorous task
required of their office especially in the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Local Development Council (LDC) to incorporate in the Annual
Investment Plan (AIP) programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) that are
described specifically as to name and location; and
b. Secure approval or authorization from the Local Sanggunian prior to
implementation of specific programs/projects/activities funded out of lump
sum appropriation under the 20% DF pursuant to Article 4 (d) of the Rules and
Regulation Implementing the Local Government Code and Item 3.1.1 COA
Circular No. 2012-001 as clarified in COA Memorandum 2010-014 dated April
22, 2010.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to install the required billboard/signage for
ongoing projects; and
b. Strictly comply with the transparency requirements of the 1987 Constitution as
specified in Section 2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 in
all its infrastructure projects undertaken either by straight contract or by
administration.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit as-built plans for those
completed projects, and approved plans/drawings for on-going and
unimplemented infrastructure projects; and
b. Henceforth, strictly comply with COA Circular 2009-001 on the timely
submission of copy government contracts, purchase orders, and their
supporting documents to facilitate auditorial review.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Revert the unexpended balance or savings from CY 2014-2016 continuing
appropriation to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent
re-appropriation by the Local Sanggunian for other social services;
b. Utilize the unexpended balance or savings from CY 2017-2018 continuing
appropriation for other relevant disaster risk reduction and management
activities of the Municipality as provided for in the LDRRMFIP or in its revised
plan;
c. Implement the program under the CY 2014 continuing appropriation which has
been long overdue, otherwise, reprogram the funds to a more feasible and
realizable programs and projects; and
d. Henceforth, ensure strict and timely implementation of programs, projects, and
activities embodied in the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit a request for write-off
pertaining to the dormant receivables amounting to ₱34,801.11; and
b. Henceforth, strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016 on the procedures for the write-off of dormant receivable accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned official to prepare,
maintain, and attach the documentary requirements prescribed in Section
9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the disbursements
for repairs and maintenance of equipment and motor vehicle; and
b. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the limitations set forth in Par. 11, Annex C and
Par. 2, Annex D of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy between the balances in the books and per physical count of the PPE accounts;
e. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain complete property and ledger cards as required in Sections 114 and 120, Volume 1 of the NGAS for LGUs;

f. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct regularly a year-end physical inventory on Property, Plant and Equipment and submit it to the Accounting Unit for reconciliation of balances to ensure correctness and reliability of the PPE account balances;
g. Require the GSO to annually provide a copy of the reconciled RPCPPE to the COA, not later than January 31st of the ensuing year, showing therein the proper classification of PPE; and
observations.managementAction: The GSO was able to submit the annual RPCPPE, however, the same was not reconciled with the Property Ledger Cards/FS of the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: h. If possible, provide additional manpower to assist the GSO in performing the rigorous task required of their office.
observations.managementAction: The Management designated additional personnel to assist the GSO
observations.reason: however, the existing number of manpower is still not enough to undertake the task.

observations.recommendation: a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified prioritized development Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 20% Development Fund (DF), so that any cause of suspension or problems that would delay the project implementation would be immediately addressed and more desired benefits could be delivered to the constituents;
observations.managementAction: Utilization rate of 81% was noted in CY 2021 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: b. Prepare and be specific in the identification of projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan that have undergone priority ranking by the Local Development Council which are attainable within the desired time frame to maximize the allocated funds that have an immediate impact on the development of the Municipality this time of pandemic; and
observations.managementAction: The CY 2021 AIP still includes generic or lumpsum PPAs. This observation was covered under AOM No. 2022-002 dated March 30, 2022 and reiterated in this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: c. Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental management and disaster response requirements provided by DILG - DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG - DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020.
a. Properly plan, closely monitor, and strictly implement Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 5% Gender and Development Fund so that problems/issues may be immediately addressed;
observations.managementAction: Utilization rate of 79% was noted in CY 2021 GAD Fund

observations.recommendation: b. Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable gender issues and significant PPAs this time of COVID-19 pandemic; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004.1 in formulating PPAs in order to address gender issues and achieve maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Properly plan, closely monitor, and evaluate the implementation of the identified and prioritized development Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 20% Development Fund (DF), so that any cause of suspension or problems that would delay the project implementation would be immediately addressed and more desired benefits could be delivered to the constituents;
Prepare and be specific in the identification of projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan that have undergone priority ranking by the Local Development Council which are attainable within the desired time frame to maximize the allocated funds that have an immediate impact on the development of the Municipality this time of pandemic; and
Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable and significant PPAs in accordance with the socio-economic development, environmental management and disaster response requirements provided by DILG - DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG - DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Properly plan, closely monitor, and strictly implement Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) funded by the 5% Gender and Development Fund so that problems/issues may be immediately addressed;
Review unimplemented PPAs and ascertain viability of implementation, otherwise, realign or reprogram for more viable gender issues and significant PPAs this time of COVID-19 pandemic; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DBM, NEDA, and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004.1 in formulating PPAs in order to address gender issues and achieve maximum benefits for its constituents.

Direct the GAD Focal Person to fast track the creation and maintenance of GAD Database containing sex-disaggregated data pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and

Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget.

Require the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the registration of all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and

Cause the cancellation of the three (3) Tax Declaration still in the name of the former owners and transfer it in the name of the LGU.

observations.reason: RPT Assessment were done manually, thus, caused delay in generating the report/ list.

observations.recommendation:
Advise the Administrative Officers and other Approving Officials to perform proper review prior to approval of Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction, making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum required by the law; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to include a phrase stating that the Certificate of Net Pay of Employees is not applicable for loan purposes, or any other similarly applicable notes before signing the Certificate of Net Pay of Employees and upon determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by law, in order to strictly implement related provisions and restrictions of the law.
b. Cause the cancellation of the three (3) Tax Declaration still in the name of the former owners and transfer it in the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: The management is still on the process of titling of all the lots owned by the Municipality. According to them, they are now waiting for the release of titles for some lots while the processing of others are still pending due to some legal issues and circumstances beyond their control such as land disputes and existence of decrees which hinders the process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the GAD Focal Person to fast track the creation and maintenance of GAD
Database containing sex-disaggregated data pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2013-01; and
b. Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender
analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the
formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommend that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the registration of all real estate owned
by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386;
and
b. Cause the cancellation of the three (3) Tax Declaration still in the name of the former
owners and transfer it in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that management:
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to comply with his duties and functions as stipulated
in Section 472 of the Local Government Code and Manual on Real Property
Appraisal and Assessment Operations;
b. Provide the Municipal Assessor’s Office with a qualified and able tax mapper as well
as the adequate manpower to do the tax mapping activity, ocular inspection and
other relevant functions of the said office;
c. Submit updated Report on Assessment to the Provincial Assessor’s Office to provide
them an accurate information relevant to their plan for a possible general revision;
and
d. Furnish the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the updated and accurate data on RPT
Assessment which serves as basis for RPT target and collection every year.

We recommended that:
a. Advise the Administrative Officers and other Approving Officials to perform proper
review prior to approval of Loan Applications of employees under their jurisdiction,
making sure that after amortizations of the loan applied for are deducted, the same
will not bring the Net Take Home Pay of the employee to below the minimum
required by the law; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to include a phrase stating that the Certificate of Net
Pay of Employees is not applicable for loan purposes, or any other similarly
applicable notes before signing the Certificate of Net Pay of Employees and upon
determining that the Net Take Home Pay will fall below what is required by law, in
order to strictly implement related provisions and restrictions of the law.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to submit the required documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period
to facilitate the auditorial review and technical evaluation of the same as well as the
timely preparation of the necessary reports.

Likewise, BAC should be reminded that the submission of required documents as
attachment to DV’s does not relieve them from their duty to submit a copy of the same to
the COA Audit Team.
a. Effect full and timely implementation of the projects embodied in the AIP;
b. Make immediate realignment, if planned programs and projects are no longer feasible or could not be implemented, so that the amount will be utilized for other relevant projects; and
c. Subject the project to straight contract so as not to hamper the full implementation of the project.
a. Initiate the creation and maintenance of GAD Database containing sex-disaggregated data pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower, the concerned department is still on the process of gathering data.

observations.recommendation: b. Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget;
observations.reason: Due to absence of GAD Database, gender analysis and assessment cannot be performed.

observations.recommendation: c. Ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided pursuant to Section 36 of Republic Act No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the registration of all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and
a. Require the Municipal Assessor to comply with his duties and functions as stipulated in Section 472 of the Local Government Code and Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations;
observations.reason: The management is still on the process of hiring and training of proper personnel to do the vital tasks and functions in the Assessor’s Office

observations.recommendation:
b. Submit updated Quarterly Report on Assessment to the Provincial Assessor’s Office to provide them an accurate information relevant to their plan for a possible general revision; and
c. Furnish the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the updated and accurate data on RPT Assessment which serves as basis for RPT target and collection every year.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Effect full and timely implementation of the projects embodied in the AIP;
Make immediate realignment, if planned programs and projects are no longer feasible or could not be implemented, so that the amount will be utilized for other relevant projects; and
Subject the project to straight contract so as not to hamper the full implementation of the project.

We recommended that the Management:
Initiate the creation and maintenance of GAD Database containing sex-disaggregated data pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
Establish GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues using the results of gender analysis and assessment provided by sex-disaggregated data as basis for the formulation of PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget; and
Ensure that the programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided pursuant to Section 36 of Republic Act No. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Assessor to fast-track the registration of all real estate owned by municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386; and
Cause the cancellation of the three (3) Tax Declaration still in the name of the former owners and transfer it in the name of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Assessor to comply with his duties and functions as stipulated in Section 472 of the Local Government Code and Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations; and
b.	Submit updated Quarterly Report on Assessment to the Provincial Assessor’s Office to provide them an accurate information relevant to their plan for a possible general revision in 2020.

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annexes I-I5 which are already overdue; and 

Henceforth, strictly comply with sections 6.03, 6.04 and 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate timely auditorial review.

We recommended that Management:
Establish a Municipal Council for the Protection of Children;
Allocate at least 1% of their IRA for Programs, Projects and Activities for children’s welfare; and
Strictly comply with the rules and regulations about the formulation and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

We recommended that the Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the accounts and reports listed in Annexes I-I5 which are already overdue; and
b.	Henceforth, strictly comply with sections 6.03, 6.04 and 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 on the timely submission of financial reports and records of receipts and disbursements to facilitate timely auditorial review.

We recommended that Management:
Present the details of specific projects and activities to be undertaken in the Annual Budget;
Explore and implement other viable initiatives to increase collection from SWM services to generate sufficient amount needed for SWM programs; and
Procure and utilize the fund allocated for SWM in accordance with Section 6 of Rule XV, Part IV and Section 3.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the suggested sample format under Section 5.1.2 of the Circular; and

Direct the MDRRMO to ensure the submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Provincial Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Present the details of the specific projects and activities to be undertaken in the Annual Budget; and

Procure and utilize the fund allocated for SWM sourced from the 20% DF in accordance with Section 6 of Rule XV, Part IV and DILG-DBM JMC No, 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

To effect the timely implementation of the projects embodied in the AIP

On cases there is an unutilized/remaining amount left for every program/projects, realignment will be made to a program/project that need immediate attention/action

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR;
b. Strengthen internal control over procurement activities especially in the role of
the BAC;
c. Make payments thru checks and only payments in small amounts may be
made by cash through the petty cash fund. See to it all receipts were deducted
with corresponding withholding tax; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes due the government in
accordance with the BIR regulations for those official receipts not deducted with
corresponding withholding tax.

Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) No. 2017-002 dated January 27, 2017 was
issued for this observation.

The Management suggested that Petty Cash Fund be increased so as they can procure
at any point in time to cater parts and materials needed for the repair of the heavy
equipment.

The Audit Team emphasized the need to abide with the RA if the procurement will
involve big amount.

We recommended that Management:
a. Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the suggested sample
format under Section 5.1.2 of the Circular; and
b. Direct the MDRRMO to ensure the submission of the Report on Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor duly certified by the Provincial
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to
Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

AOM No. 2017-003 dated January 30, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The Management gave assurance that effective 2017 they will submit the report
monthly.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the AO and the Accountant to prepare and submit required reports
namely; RCI, the ROD, with all the originals of vouchers/payrolls and all
supporting documents showing utilization of funds within fifteen (15) days after
the end of each month;
b. Include all the projects, programs and activities under the Fund Transfers in the
Quarterly Accomplishment Report submitted by the Municipality to be subject
for inspection and validation, or upon completion submit written request for
inspection to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Cause immediate refund of the unutilized balance of the Fund Transfers to the
SA concerned.

AOM No. 2017-006 dated February 28, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The Management gave assurance to implement the recommendation in 2017.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly implement and monitor the imposition of fines and penalties
enumerated in the municipal ordinance as mandated under RA 9003;
b. Ensure proper issuance of Citation Tickets to violators to yield increase in
revenue and effectively maintain cleanliness in the Municipality; and
c. Post tarpaulins/billboards in conspicuous places within the Municipality
indicating the ordinance and the penal sanctions in case of violation so that the
public may be informed.

AOM No. 2017-001 dated January 25, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The Management admitted that the same was not given priority. However, they gave
assurance to implement the recommendation in 2017.

We recommended that Management:
a. Present the details of specific projects and activities to be undertaken in the
Annual Budget; and
b. Procure and utilize the fund allocated for SWM sourced from the 20% DF in
accordance with with Section 6 of Rule XV, Part IV and DILG-DBM JMC No,
2011-1 dated April 13, 2011

AOM No. 2017-001 dated January 25, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The Management admitted that the same was not given priority. However, they gave
assurance to implement the recommendation in 2017

We recommended that Management:
a. Effect full and timely implementation of the projects embodied in the AIP; and
b. Make immediate realignment, if planned programs and projects are no longer
feasible or could not be implemented, so that the amount will be utilized for
other relevant projects.

AOM No. 2017-004 dated January 31, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The Management assured the Audit Team that they effect full and timely implementation
of the projects as embodied in the AIP.

We recommended that Management:
a. Ensure that the utilization of the GAD Fund is in accordance with the approved
Annual GAD Plan and Budget aligned with DBM, NEDA, and NCRWW Joint
Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004; and
b. Prepare accordingly a revised annual GAD Plan and Budget to reformulate
existing or incorporate additional programs, projects and activities intended to
effectively address relevant gender issues within the Municipality.

AOM No. 2017-005 dated February 15, 2017 was issued for this observation.

The management assured the Audit Team that they will utilize the GAD fund in
accordance with approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the personnel in charge of preparing the monthly payrolls to effect adjustments on deductions so that the monthly net take home pay of all employees are not lower than P3,000.00 pursuant to the provisions of Section 48 of the General Provisions of the GAA of FY 2015; and
b. Inform the personnel concerned to make proper arrangement with the lending institutions for the adjustment of employees’ loan repayments.
